summary: American novelist. Best known for Blood Meridian (the Kid, Judge Holden, Glanton Gang), All the Pretty Horses (John Grady Cole, Alejandra, Border Trilogy), and his Appalachian novels (Child of God, Outer Dark). Also wrote The Passenger and Stella Maris (2022). First four novels set in Appalachia; later work set in the American Southwest/Mexico.

works.name: Blood Meridian
works.description: Western epic (1985) subtitled "The Evening Redness of the West." The Kid joins the Glanton Gang under the terrifying Judge Holden.

clues.clue: The Kid joins the Glanton Gang under Judge Holden — giant, hairless man who claims he will never die
examples: protagonist joins the Glanton Gang under Judge Holden
a tall, hairless character who furiously plays the fiddle and proclaims that he will never die

clues.clue: Judge Holden dances in a bar under a meteor shower, proclaiming he will never die; murders a man in the jakes
examples: claims he will never die while dancing in a bar under a meteor shower
murders a man in the jakes

clues.clue: Alternately titled "The Evening Redness of the West"
examples: alternately titled 'The Evening Redness of the West'

works.name: All the Pretty Horses
works.description: First novel of the Border Trilogy. John Grady Cole runs away to Mexico from his dead grandfather's ranch with friend Rawlins; has affair with Alejandra.

clues.clue: John Grady Cole runs away to Mexico with friend Rawlins; has affair with Alejandra, a ranch owner's daughter
examples: the protagonist has an affair with Alejandra after running away to Mexico from his dead grandfather's ranch
John Grady Cole in All the Pretty Horses

clues.clue: 13-year-old claims to be deaf in one ear after being struck by lightning; shoots hole through wallet tossed in air
examples: a 13-year-old character who claims to be deaf in one ear after being struck by lightning twice

clues.clue: John Grady stabs a cuchillero to death in prison knife fight with cafeteria tray after discussing countries with Perez
examples: stabs a cuchillero to death during a knife and cafeteria tray fight in prison

clues.clue: Uses heated pistol barrel to cauterize a gunshot wound from prison escape
examples: uses a heated pistol barrel to cauterize a gunshot wound

works.name: Child of God
works.description: Novel about Lester Ballard, a serial killer in Appalachian Tennessee who keeps women's bodies in a cave.

clues.clue: Lester Ballard — serial killer "much like yourself perhaps"; lives in a mountain cave keeping bodies of murdered women
examples: created a serial killer 'much like yourself perhaps' named Lester Ballard
moves into a mountainous cave, where he begins keeping the bodies of women he murders

works.name: The Border Trilogy
works.description: All the Pretty Horses, The Crossing, and Cities of the Plain.

clues.clue: The Crossing — protagonist cries after failing to call back injured dog; begins with returning a wolf to the mountains
examples: cries after failing to call back a badly injured dog he had shooed away

clues.clue: Cities of the Plain — protagonist falls in love with epileptic prostitute at The White Lake; killed in knife fight
examples: falls in love with an epileptic prostitute working at The White Lake, whose owner kills him in a knife fight

works.name: Outer Dark
works.description: Novel in which three unnamed characters burn a child and hang a character known as the Tinker.

clues.clue: Three unnamed characters burn a child in same site where they hang the Tinker
examples: three unnamed characters burn a child in the same site where they hang a character known as the Tinker

works.name: The Passenger / Stella Maris
works.description: 2022 companion novels about two mathematical prodigies whose father worked on the atomic bomb.

clues.clue: The Passenger (2022) and Stella Maris ("coda") — two mathematical prodigies; sister hallucinates the Thalidomide Kid; father worked on the atomic bomb
examples: Stella Maris was written as a 'coda' to The Passenger
two mathematical prodigies whose father worked on the atomic bomb

clues.clue: First four novels set in Appalachia; later work set in American Southwest and Mexico
examples: set their first four novels in Appalachia

clues.clue: Coen brothers adapted No Country for Old Men and wrote Barton Fink partly modeled on him
examples: The Coen brothers modeled Barton Fink on this playwright

comprehensive_summary: Cormac McCarthy is an American novelist known for violent, lyrical fiction. Blood Meridian (1985), subtitled "The Evening Redness of the West," follows the Kid as he joins the Glanton Gang under the terrifying Judge Holden, a giant hairless man who dances under a meteor shower proclaiming he will never die and murders a man in the jakes. All the Pretty Horses, the first of the Border Trilogy, follows John Grady Cole who runs away to Mexico with friend Rawlins and has an affair with Alejandra. The Crossing begins with returning a wolf to the mountains, and Cities of the Plain ends with the protagonist killed in a knife fight. Child of God follows Lester Ballard, a serial killer who keeps bodies in a cave. Outer Dark features three unnamed figures who burn a child. The Passenger and Stella Maris (2022) follow mathematical prodigies whose father worked on the atomic bomb.

cards.front: Novel: the Kid joins the Glanton Gang under Judge Holden, a giant hairless man who dances under a meteor shower proclaiming he will never die
Novel: John Grady Cole runs away to Mexico from his dead grandfather's ranch with friend Rawlins
Novel: has affair with Alejandra, a ranch owner's daughter
Novel: serial killer "much like yourself perhaps" named Lester Ballard lives in a mountain cave keeping the bodies of women he murders
Novel: 2022 companion novels about mathematical prodigies whose father worked on the atomic bomb
Novel: sister hallucinates the Thalidomide Kid
